name: Busecca Ticinese (Tripe Soup with Garlic Brea
recipeInstructions:
-----------------------GARLIC BREAD-----------------------
200 grams French bread (7 oz)
50 grams butter (1.75 oz)
4 cloves garlic, mashed
10 grams Gruyere cheese, grated (0.25 oz)
1 tablespoon parsley
Salt and freshly ground pepper
Preparation: 30 minutes, cooking time: 3 hours
Yields 6 servings as a first course, or serve separately as the main course
Double the ingredients if desired.
1. Soak beans overnight.
2. Drain beans.
3. Cook in lightly salted water until tender.
4. Drain and set aside.
5. Clean, trim, and wash vegetables (carrots, leek, celery).
6. Slice carrots finely, slice leek diagonally, and slice celery finely into squares.
7. Cut tripe into strips 4 cm long and 2 mm wide.
8. Fill a kettle with water, add salt, bring to a boil.
9. Add tripe and cook for 2 1/2 hours.
10. Drain the tripe.
11. In a saucepan, heat butter. Sauté vegetables briefly.
12. Add tripe, garlic, and tomato paste; sauté briefly.
13. Moisten with bouillon and simmer for 20 minutes.
14. Season with caraway seeds, thyme, salt, and pepper.
15. Add Borlotti beans and heat.
16. Serve in soup plates, sprinkling with Parmesan cheese or serve separately.
17. Garnish with parsley.
Garlic bread:
1. Cut bread into slices and toast in an oven.
2. Beat butter until creamy.
3. Chop parsley finely and blend with garlic, Gruyere cheese, and parsley.
4. Season with salt and pepper.
5. Spread the butter mixture on the bread.
6. Gratin under a broiler until golden brown.
7. Serve with soup.
